LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. RESPONDENTS CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF FISH AND WILDLIFE AND CALIFORNIA FISH AND GAME COMMISSION'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED AS TO THE THIRD CLAIM FOR VIOLATIONS OF THE CONSTITUTIONAL EXCESSIVE FINES CLAUSES AND DENIED AS TO THE REMAINDER OF PETITIONER'S CLAIMS. THE EXCESSIVE FINES CLAUSES DO NOT APPLY TO THE REVOCATION OF A GOVERNMENT-ISSUED PERMIT. FISH AND GAME CODE SECTION 8279.1, STRICTLY CONSTRUED AS IT MUST BE SINCE ITS VIOLATION RESULTS IN THE FORFEITURE OF AN IMPORTANT PROPERTY RIGHT RELATING TO THE CARRYING ON OF AN OCCUPATION, ONLY PROHIBITS LANDING OF CRAB THAT WERE ILLEGALLY TAKEN FROM OCEAN WATERS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MILES R. LENDWAY, CSR # 13684 =302/HK

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Oct-30-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. E. MICHAEL LINSCHEID, TEL. NO. 1-510-451-2010 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. JOHN G. YOUNG OF THE WILLIAMS KASTNER LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-206-628-6660 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. RESPONDENTS CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF FISH AND WILDLIFE AND CALIFORNIA FISH AND GAME COMMISSION'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Defendant's motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted as to the third claim for violations of the constitutional excessive fines clauses and denied as to the remainder of petitioner's claims. The excessive fines clauses do not apply to the revocation of a government-issued permit. Fish and Game code section 8279.1, strictly construed as it must be since its violation results in the forfeiture of an important property right relating to the carrying on of an occupation, only prohibits landing of crab that were illegally taken from ocean waters. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Miles R. Lendway, CSR # 13684 =302/hk
